rule_1.condition: personal data breach occurred

rule_2.condition: when transferring personal data to a third country or an international organisation
rule_2.raw_text: The controller or processor shall ensure that the level of protection of natural persons guaranteed by this Regulation is not undermined when transferring personal data to a third country or an international organisation.

reasoning: Rule 1 imposes an obligation for a controller to notify in the event of a personal data breach. Rule 2, on the other hand, imposes an obligation on the controller or processor to ensure appropriate safeguards when transferring personal data to a third country or international organization. However, only one of these obligations can be fulfilled at any given time. This creates a conflict between the two rules. The application of Rule 1 requires the controller to notify in the event of a personal data breach, which may interrupt or delay the transfer of personal data under Rule 2. On the other hand, the application of Rule 2 requires the controller to ensure appropriate safeguards when transferring personal data to a third country or international organization, which may involve additional time and resources that may compromise the controller's ability to fulfill its obligation under Rule 1 to notify in the event of a personal data breach within the required timeframe.

rule_1.entity: body as referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article
rule_1.condition: in cases of infringement of the code by a controller or processor
rule_1.scope: includes suspension or exclusion of the controller or processor concerned from the code
rule_1.raw_text: (1) Subject to appropriate safeguards, a body as referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall take appropriate action in cases of infringement of the code by a controller or processor. Such actions may include suspension or exclusion of the controller or processor concerned from the code. (2) It shall inform the competent supervisory authority of such actions and the reasons for taking them.

rule_2.condition: when transferring personal data to a third country or an international organisation

rule_1.action: request confirmation of data processing activities, or access to their personal data
rule_1.condition: the data subject has made a written, electronic or oral request
rule_1.raw_text: Data subjects have the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether or not personal data concerning them are being processed. They also have the right to request from the controller access to their personal data and the following information: (a) the purposes of the processing; (b) the categories of personal data concerned; (c) the recipients or categories of recipient to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed, in particular recipients in third countries or international organisations; (d) where possible, the envisaged period for which the personal data will be stored, or, if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period; (e) the existence of the right to request from the controller rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing of personal data concerning the data subject or to object to such processing; (f) the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority; (g) where the personal data are not collected from the data subject, any available information as to their source; (h) the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, referred to in Article 22(1) and (4) and, at least in those cases, meaningful information about the logic involved, as well as the significance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject.

rule_2.condition: in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient
rule_2.raw_text: The international organisation or third country that provides adequate data protection in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient shall be considered an exception in relation to specific international data transfers.

reasoning: Rule 1 allows a data subject to request confirmation or access to their personal data. Rule 2 allows an international organisation or third country to provide personal data when adequate data protection is in place. The conflict arises from the potential interaction between data subjects' rights to access their personal data under Rule 1 and the international organisation or third country's ability to transfer personal data in accordance with Rule 2.

rule_1.condition: high risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects
rule_1.raw_text: Where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data.

rule_1.action: transparently determine respective responsibilities for compliance
rule_1.condition: compliance with the obligations under the Regulation
rule_1.raw_text: They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation, in particular as regards the exercising of the rights of the data subject and their respective duties to provide the information referred to in Articles 13 and 14, by means of an arrangement between them unless, and in so far as, the respective responsibilities of the controllers are determined by Union or Member State law to which the controllers are subject. The arrangement may designate a contact point for data subjects.

rule_2.condition: in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient
rule_2.raw_text: The international organisation or third country that provides adequate data protection in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient shall be considered an exception in relation to specific international data transfers.

reasoning: Rule 1 defines joint controllers and their obligation to comply with the GDPR. Rule 2 defines the obligations for data transfer to international organizations or third countries in accordance with the International Data Transfer Regulation. The two rules contain shared concepts, such as regulation, the controllers, and this regulation. However, the rules also contain conflicting conditions for compliance. Rule 1 requires compliance with the GDPR, which may place restrictions on data transfer to international organizations or third countries as defined in Rule 2. Rule 2 allows for data transfer to international organizations or third countries when providing adequate data protection and in accordance with certain legal instruments. However, Rule 2 does not specify which legal instruments are applicable and binding for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient. This creates a potential conflict between the obligations to comply with the GDPR as defined in Rule 1 and the requirements for adequate data protection and compliance with certain legal instruments as defined in Rule 2.

rule_1.condition: no longer requires identification of data subject
rule_1.raw_text: If the purposes for which a controller processes personal data do not or do no longer require the identification of a data subject by the controller, the controller shall not be obliged to maintain, acquire or process additional information in order to identify the data subject for the sole purpose of complying with this Regulation.

rule_2.condition: in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient
rule_2.raw_text: The international organisation or third country that provides adequate data protection in accordance with this Regulation or another legal instrument with binding effect for both the controller or processor and the controller or recipient shall be considered an exception in relation to specific international data transfers.

Rule 2 allows for the international organisation or third country to provide adequate data protection in accordance with the regulation. The exception in Rule 2 allows for the transfer of personal data to the international organisation or third country despite the absence of identification of the data subject. Therefore, the relationship between the two rules is one of exception.

rule_2.condition: the data transfer is not repetitive, concerns only a limited number of data subjects, and is necessary for important reasons of public interest
rule_2.raw_text: The controller shall inform the supervisory authority of the transfer and the data subject of the transfer and on the compelling legitimate interests pursued. A transfer pursuant to point (g) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall not involve the entirety of the personal data or entire categories of the personal data contained in the register. Where the register is intended for consultation by persons having a legitimate interest, the controller shall ensure that the consultation does not result in the disclosure of personal data to unauthorised third parties. An inadvertent disclosure of personal data, which is made as a result of the application of a law, including a statute of limitation, shall not be considered a violation of this Regulation.
